user.title: Presence Conductor
user.description: Robust occupancy estimation for mmWave presence sensors. Pick the radar devices the conductor should estimate from — you will slice each one into zones in the next steps.

data_description.sensors: Devices exposing the LD2410 radar entity cluster (move/still energy plus distance), found in your entity registry. Apollo MSR-2 devices show up here automatically.
data_description.add_manual: Turn on to assign a sensor's entities by hand — for radars whose entities do not follow the standard naming.

manual_sensor.title: Manual sensor
manual_sensor.description: Assign the entities of one mmWave device to their roles. Energies and both distances are required; the binary target entities are optional and only sharpen the estimate. Per-gate energy entities (g0-g8, engineering mode) are picked up automatically for discovered sensors only; manually assigned sensors run on the aggregate energies.

data_description.name: Display name for the device. It also becomes the sensor's internal ID, so it must be unique.
data_description.moving_distance: Distance to the moving target, in cm.
data_description.still_distance: Distance to the still target, in cm.
data_description.target: Binary presence output of the radar (optional).
data_description.add_another: Turn on to assign one more sensor after this one.

zones.title: Zone {zone_number} for {sensor} (sensor {sensor_number} of {sensor_count})
zones.description: A zone is a distance slice of this sensor's beam and the unit of estimation. The default is one zone covering the full range; give it the room it belongs to. Zones of different sensors that share a room are fused into one room occupancy.

data.name: Zone name
data.add_another_zone: Add another zone for this sensor

data_description.name: Display name for the zone. It also becomes the zone's internal ID, so it must be unique.
data_description.room: Zones with the same room name fuse into one room occupancy — pick an area or type any name. Same text = same room.
data_description.near_cm: Nearest distance that belongs to this zone, in cm.
data_description.far_cm: Farthest distance that belongs to this zone, in cm (600 is the radar's full range).
data_description.fallback: Evidence without a usable distance reading is attributed to this zone. At most one per sensor; without one, the sensor's nearest zone is used.
data_description.add_another_zone: Turn on to slice another zone out of this sensor's range.

overlap.title: Overlapping zones

error.no_sensors_selected: Select at least one sensor or turn on manual assignment.
error.invalid_sensor_name: The sensor name must contain at least one letter or digit.
error.duplicate_sensor_name: Another sensor already uses this name. Sensor names must be unique.
error.invalid_zone_name: The zone name must contain at least one letter or digit.
error.duplicate_zone_name: Another zone already uses this name. Zone names must be unique.
error.invalid_room_name: The room name must contain at least one letter or digit.
error.invalid_zone_range: The far edge must be greater than the near edge.
error.invalid_attack_gap: Attack gap maximum must be greater than attack gap minimum.
error.multiple_fallback_zones: This sensor already has a fallback zone. At most one zone per sensor can be the fallback.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Presence Conductor is already set up. Only a single instance is supported — use Configure on the existing entry instead.

init.title: Presence Conductor options
init.description: Pick a section to edit. Changes apply when the section is saved; recorded baselines are preserved.

menu_options.sensors: Sensors — add or remove mmWave devices

sensors.title: Sensors
sensors.description: Currently configured sensors are preselected; deselect to remove them (their zones are removed too). Newly discovered devices appear unselected — select to add them; you will define their zones next.

data_description.sensors: Configured sensors and newly discovered radar devices.
data_description.add_manual: Turn on to assign a sensor's entities by hand.

manual_sensor.title: Manual sensor
manual_sensor.description: Assign the entities of one mmWave device to their roles. Energies and both distances are required; the binary target entities are optional and only sharpen the estimate. Per-gate energy entities (g0-g8, engineering mode) are picked up automatically for discovered sensors only; manually assigned sensors run on the aggregate energies.

data_description.name: Display name for the device. It also becomes the sensor's internal ID, so it must be unique.
data_description.moving_distance: Distance to the moving target, in cm.
data_description.still_distance: Distance to the still target, in cm.
data_description.target: Binary presence output of the radar (optional).
data_description.add_another: Turn on to assign one more sensor after this one.

new_zones.title: Zone {zone_number} for {sensor} (new sensor {sensor_number} of {sensor_count})
new_zones.description: Define the zones of the newly added sensor. The default is one zone covering the full range; give it the room it belongs to.

data.name: Zone name
data.add_another_zone: Add another zone for this sensor

data_description.name: Display name for the zone. It also becomes the zone's internal ID, so it must be unique.
data_description.room: Zones with the same room name fuse into one room occupancy — pick an area or type any name. Same text = same room.
data_description.near_cm: Nearest distance that belongs to this zone, in cm.
data_description.far_cm: Farthest distance that belongs to this zone, in cm (600 is the radar's full range).
data_description.fallback: Evidence without a usable distance reading is attributed to this zone. At most one per sensor; without one, the sensor's nearest zone is used.
data_description.add_another_zone: Turn on to slice another zone out of this sensor's range.

zones.title: Zone {zone_number} of {zone_count}: {zone}
zones.description: Adjust this zone's room and distance slice. Current settings are prefilled; recorded baselines are preserved.

data_description.room: Zones with the same room name fuse into one room occupancy — pick an area or type any name. Same text = same room.
data_description.near_cm: Nearest distance that belongs to this zone, in cm.
data_description.far_cm: Farthest distance that belongs to this zone, in cm (600 is the radar's full range).
data_description.fallback: Evidence without a usable distance reading is attributed to this zone. At most one per sensor; without one, the sensor's nearest zone is used.

overlap.title: Overlapping zones

tunables.title: Tunables
tunables.description: Behavioral tuning knobs of the estimation engine. The defaults mirror docs/ENGINE_SPEC.md; current values are prefilled.

data_description.margin_cm: Zone mask slack absorbing the radar's interpolated-distance smoothing.
data_description.stale_after: Silence window before an occupied sensor's zones go to unknown health.
data_description.tick_interval: Expected tick cadence driving decay, dwell and holds.
data_description.sigma_min: The calibrated noise-floor sigma is never below this (normalized energy units).
data_description.energy_quantum: Device energy reporting step (normalized); half a step guards scale estimates against grid bias.
data_description.default_mu: Baseline mean used for zones without a recorded baseline.
data_description.default_sigma: Baseline sigma used for zones without a recorded baseline.
data_description.z_cap: Upper cap on the centered evidence score, so one frame can never overwhelm the filter.
data_description.z_neg_cap: Lower cap on the centered evidence score — bounds how fast absence evidence can drive a zone down.
data_description.stat_sigma_min: The statistic calibration's deviation is never below this (raw statistic units); the analytic reference deviation floors it as well.
data_description.stat_min_rows: Distinct calibration observations required before floors or statistics are replaced.
data_description.tau_int_max: Upper clamp on the estimated integrated autocorrelation time of the empty process.
data_description.obs_budget: How long a positive score integrates after its observation — a held value is one measurement.
data_description.obs_hold: How long a non-positive score and the absence bias integrate after the last observation.
data_description.use_gate_evidence: Score per-gate energies (experimental until real gate-path timing is validated); off = validated aggregate path.
data_description.k_move: Per-second evidence weight of the move channel.
data_description.k_still: Per-second evidence weight of the still channel.
data_description.k_bias: Per-second bias always subtracted from the evidence rate, keeping a calibrated empty room drifting down, never up.
data_description.u_cap: Upward cap on the per-second evidence rate — one wild sample must not out-accumulate a genuine entry.
data_description.tau_decay: Relaxation time constant of the belief toward the empty prior (the departure hazard — there is no fixed occupancy timeout).
data_description.p_prior: Empty-state prior confidence the belief decays toward.
data_description.attack_tail_ppm: Nominal per-observation Gaussian tail, in parts per million, of the raw move statistic that makes it an attack candidate. It is analytic and gate-count corrected, not a distribution-free false-alarm guarantee.
data_description.attack_confirm: Qualifying frames needed to fire the fast attack (1 restores single-frame attack).
data_description.attack_gap_min: Minimum spacing between the candidate and the confirming frame.
data_description.attack_gap_max: Maximum spacing before the attack candidate expires.
data_description.p_attack: Confidence floor applied immediately on a confirmed fast attack (the lights-on path).
data_description.theta_on: Occupied turns on at this confidence.
data_description.theta_off: Occupied turns off at this confidence; between the thresholds the state holds.
data_description.z_motion: Centered move score that turns the raw motion output on.
data_description.motion_hold: How long motion stays on after the last motion evidence.
data_description.p_min: Lower belief clamp, so long emptiness cannot build unbounded inertia.
data_description.p_max: Upper belief clamp, so long occupation cannot build unbounded inertia.
data_description.distance_hold: How long a frozen distance stays usable after its target flag was last on.
data_description.p_background: Background adaptation runs only while the zone's confidence stays below this.
data_description.t_background: How long the confidence must stay below the gate before adaptation starts.
data_description.tau_background: EMA time constant of the slow background adaptation.
data_description.baseline_duration: Default collection window of a Record baseline run.
data_description.t_dwell: Continuous occupancy needed before passing becomes active.
data_description.t_settle: Still-channel dominance needed before the zone counts as settled.
data_description.tau_home: Decay time constant of anyone-home once every zone is empty — deliberately slow, because the sensors do not cover every room.
data_description.theta_home_on: Anyone-home turns on at this confidence.
data_description.theta_home_off: Anyone-home turns off at this confidence.

error.no_sensors_selected: Select at least one sensor or turn on manual assignment.
error.invalid_sensor_name: The sensor name must contain at least one letter or digit.
error.duplicate_sensor_name: Another sensor already uses this name. Sensor names must be unique.
error.invalid_zone_name: The zone name must contain at least one letter or digit.
error.duplicate_zone_name: Another zone already uses this name. Zone names must be unique.
error.invalid_room_name: The room name must contain at least one letter or digit.
error.invalid_zone_range: The far edge must be greater than the near edge.
error.invalid_attack_gap: Attack gap maximum must be greater than attack gap minimum.
error.multiple_fallback_zones: This sensor already has a fallback zone. At most one zone per sensor can be the fallback.

abort.no_zones_configured: There are no zones to edit. Add sensors first.

record_baseline.name: Record baseline
record_baseline.description: Records the empty-room noise floor of one zone. You assert the zone is empty for the window; robust statistics shrug off brief violations. The result replaces the zone's calibration and persists across restarts.

zone_id.name: Zone
zone_id.description: The zone's internal ID: the slug of its zone name (for example 'sofakrok').

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: Collection window in seconds. Defaults to the 'Baseline duration' tunable (300 s). The outcome — committed or rejected with per-channel coverage — fires as the zone's calibration event entity and the presence_conductor_baseline_recorded bus event.
